title: centrolepis racemosa
intro: Centrolepis racemosa, also known as the racemose centrolepis or New Zealand mistletoe, is a small, flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Centrolepis racemosa include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Centrolepis racemosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
